journal_entry.description: Conversion of preferred stock to common stock
journal_entry.rationale: This is an equity restructuring transaction. The conversion reduces one equity class (Preferred Stock) and increases another (Common Stock). Per IFRS, this is recorded at the carrying amount of the preferred stock being converted. The debit tuple (0,0,0,0,1,0) indicates one equity decrease line, and the credit tuple (0,1,0,0,0,0) indicates one equity increase line. No gain or loss is recognized on stock conversions between equity classes. The $100 represents the par or stated value being converted.

output.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock involves reclassifying equity accounts. The debit side decreases Preferred Stock (equity decrease), while the credit side will increase Common Stock (equity increase). This is 1 equity decrease line on the debit side.

debit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock involves reclassifying equity accounts. The debit side decreases Preferred Stock (equity decrease), while the credit side will increase Common Stock (equity increase). This is 1 equity decrease line on the debit side.

output.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock is an equity-to-equity transaction. On the credit side, we credit Common Stock (equity increase) for the par/stated value being issued. The corresponding debit decreases Preferred Stock (equity decrease). This is purely an equity reclassification with no impact on total equity, assets, liabilities, or income.

credit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock is an equity-to-equity transaction. On the credit side, we credit Common Stock (equity increase) for the par/stated value being issued. The corresponding debit decreases Preferred Stock (equity decrease). This is purely an equity reclassification with no impact on total equity, assets, liabilities, or income.
